content.text: I’ll inspect the existing filter-change implementation and API patterns to mirror the public callback shape for sorting.

I found the sort mutation path in `MudDataGrid.razor.cs`; next I’ll inspect the existing event-callback test component so I can add a temporary regression test that exercises the new event consistently with existing patterns.

5	namespace MudBlazor;
6	
7	
8	/// <summary>
9	/// Represents information about sorting in a <see cref="MudDataGrid{T}"/>.
10	/// </summary>
11	/// <typeparam name="T">The type of item being sorted.</typeparam>
12	/// <param name="SortBy">The name of the column to sort by.</param>
13	/// <param name="Descending">When <c>true</c>, sorts in descending order.</param>
14	/// <param name="Index">The order of this sort relative to other sort definitions.</param>
15	/// <param name="SortFunc">The custom function used to sort values.</param>
16	/// <param name="Comparer">The comparer used to compare values.</param>
17	public sealed record SortDefinition<T>(string SortBy, bool Descending, int Index, Func<T, object?> SortFunc, IComparer<object?>? Comparer = null);
18

file.content:
<MudDataGrid T="Item" Items="@_items" EditMode="@DataGridEditMode.Cell"
             RowClick="@OnRowClick"
             RowContextMenuClick="@OnRowContextMenuClick"
             SelectedItemChanged="@OnSelectedItemChanged"
             SelectedItemsChanged="@OnSelectedItemsChanged"
             FilterChanged="@OnFilterChanged"
             CommittedItemChanges="@OnCommittedItemChanges"
             StartedEditingItem="@OnStartedEditingItem"
             CanceledEditingItem="@OnCanceledEditingItem"
             ReadOnly="false">
    <Columns>
        <PropertyColumn Property="x => x.Name" />
    </Columns>
</MudDataGrid>

@code {
    public static string __description__ = "Used for Unit Tests to verify SelectedItem/SelectedItems Changed";

    private IEnumerable<Item> _items = new List<Item>()
    {
        new Item("A"),
        new Item("B"),
        new Item("C")
    };

    public bool RowClicked { get; set; }
    public bool RowContextMenuClicked { get; set; }
    public bool SelectedItemChanged { get; set; }
    public bool SelectedItemsChanged { get; set; }
    public bool FilterChanged { get; set; }
    public int FilterChangedCallCount { get; set; }
    public bool CommittedItemChanges { get; set; }
    public bool StartedEditingItem { get; set; }
    public bool CanceledEditingItem { get; set; }

    private void OnRowClick(DataGridRowClickEventArgs<Item> args)
    {
        RowClicked = true;
    }

    private void OnRowContextMenuClick(DataGridRowClickEventArgs<Item> args)
    {
        RowContextMenuClicked = true;
    }

    private void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item item)
    {
        SelectedItemChanged = true;
    }

    private void OnSelectedItemsChanged(HashSet<Item> items)
    {
        SelectedItemsChanged = true;
    }

    private void OnFilterChanged(IReadOnlyCollection<IFilterDefinition<Item>> _)
    {
        FilterChanged = true;
        FilterChangedCallCount++;
    }

    private Task<DataGridEditFormAction> OnCommittedItemChanges(Item item)
    {
        CommittedItemChanges = true;
        return Task.FromResult(DataGridEditFormAction.Close);
    }

    private void OnStartedEditingItem(Item item)
    {
        StartedEditingItem = true;
    }

    private void OnCanceledEditingItem(Item item)
    {
        CanceledEditingItem = true;
    }

    public class Item
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }

        public Item(string name)
        {
            Name = name;
        }
    }

}

content.content: M src/MudBlazor/Components/DataGrid/MudDataGrid.razor.cs

content.text:
Implemented the `MudDataGrid` `SortChanged` event callback.

What changed:
- Added a public `[Parameter]`:
  - `EventCallback<IReadOnlyCollection<SortDefinition<T>>> SortChanged`
- Invoked `SortChanged` whenever sort definitions are updated through the existing sort update path.
- The callback receives the current active sort definitions ordered by their sort index.

Validation:
- Temporarily added regression coverage to confirm the missing event failed before implementation.
- Confirmed the test passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test changes as requested.
- Ran existing sorting tests successfully:

```text
dotnet test src/MudBlazor.UnitTests/MudBlazor.UnitTests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MudBlazor.UnitTests.Components.DataGridTests.DataGridSortable"

Passed: 4
Failed: 0
```

Final modified file:
- `src/MudBlazor/Components/DataGrid/MudDataGrid.razor.cs`
